title: Figure 1 from: Dogan B, Duran A, Çetin Ö, Öztürk M, Martin E (2015) Study of phylogenetic relationship of Turkish species of Klasea (Asteraceae) based on ISSR amplification. PhytoKeys 56: 29-40. https://doi.org/10.3897/phytokeys.56.5608
author: Dogan, Bekir
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Figure 1 - Representative agarose gels where PCR products were amplified with the primers ISSR F5 (highest number of polymorphic bands, top) and ISSR F5 (lowest level of polymorphic bands, down). 1 Serratula tinctoria 2 Klasea quinquefolia 3 Klasea oligocephala 4 Klasea kotschyi 5 Klasea serratuloides 6 Klasea erucifolia 7 Klasea lasiocephala 8 Klasea cerinthifolia 9 Klasea grandifolia 10 Klasea radiata subsp. radiata 11 Klasea hakkiarica 12 Klasea haussknechtii 13 Klasea coriaceae 14 Klasea radiata subsp. radiata 15 Klasea kurdica 16 Klasea bornmuelleri 17 Centaurea ptosimopappoides 18 Centaurea straminicephala 19 Jurinea cataonica, M: marker.
steps_raw: Figure 1 - Representative agarose gels where PCR products were amplified with the primers ISSR F5 (highest number of polymorphic bands, top) and ISSR F5 (lowest level of polymorphic bands, down). 1 Serratula tinctoria 2 Klasea quinquefolia 3 Klasea oligocephala 4 Klasea kotschyi 5 Klasea serratuloides 6 Klasea erucifolia 7 Klasea lasiocephala 8 Klasea cerinthifolia 9 Klasea grandifolia 10 Klasea radiata subsp. radiata 11 Klasea hakkiarica 12 Klasea haussknechtii 13 Klasea coriaceae 14 Klasea radiata subsp. radiata 15 Klasea kurdica 16 Klasea bornmuelleri 17 Centaurea ptosimopappoides 18 Centaurea straminicephala 19 Jurinea cataonica, M: marker.
